cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Zenity Labs Uncovers 'AgentForger' — A ChatGPT Workspace Agent Vulnerability That Let Attackers Hijack Employee Agents
stories.plain: A single phishing link could exploit OpenAI's ChatGPT Workspace Agents to create an attacker-controlled AI agent running with a real employee's permissions and safety controls disabled. OpenAI has now patched it, but the disclosure shows that agentic AI governance gaps are a live enterprise security risk — not a theoretical one — affecting any org using Workspace Agents.

stories.head: Semrush Study: Only 15% of ChatGPT Categories Have a Consistent Brand Leader
stories.plain: Semrush data shows AI-answer brand visibility is highly unstable — in 85% of product/service categories, no single brand consistently appears as the top recommendation in ChatGPT responses. This makes GEO strategy urgent for marketers: being visible in AI answers is not yet a locked-in advantage, so the window to establish presence is open but unpredictable.

stories.head: Dstillery and Canvas Worldwide Deploy DS-1 Agentic AI for Live In-Flight Campaign Optimization
stories.plain: Dstillery's DS-1 agent now handles real-time in-flight campaign optimization on live media buys with Canvas Worldwide — moving agentic AI beyond planning and audience discovery into active budget and bid decisions. This is a meaningful shift in how media is bought: the agent acts autonomously during a running campaign, not just before launch.

stories.head: HubSpot Expands Breeze AI with Tools to Build, Govern, and Manage AI Agents
stories.plain: HubSpot's Breeze AI update adds agent-building, governance controls, and management tooling directly inside its platform — signaling that enterprise buyers now expect oversight features as standard, not add-ons. For marketers evaluating CEPs, this raises the baseline: a platform without agent governance is increasingly uncompetitive.

stories.head: Consumer Distrust of AI Is Driven More by Data Practices Than the Technology Itself
stories.plain: New research finds consumers' biggest concern with AI in marketing is how brands collect and use personal data, not the AI models themselves. For teams building personalization and agentic programs, this reframes the trust problem: improving data transparency and consent practices matters more than explaining the algorithm — and has direct implications for CDP governance strategy.

stories.head: Zenity Labs Uncovers 'AgentForger' — ChatGPT Workspace Agent Hijack Vulnerability
stories.plain: A single phishing link could silently spin up an attacker-controlled AI agent inside ChatGPT Workspace Agents, inheriting a real employee's permissions and bypassing approvals. OpenAI has now patched it, but the flaw shows that agentic AI creates a new class of insider threat that traditional security controls weren't designed to catch — a governance wake-up call for any team deploying agents.

stories.head: ChatGPT Has No Clear Brand Leader in Most Categories, Semrush Data Shows
stories.plain: Semrush analysis finds only 15% of ChatGPT product categories have a consistent brand mentioned. That means AI visibility is highly unstable — brands dominating Google search can be nearly invisible inside AI answers. For marketers, this data point reframes GEO from a nice-to-have into an urgent priority, since the AI answer layer is where buyer consideration increasingly starts.

stories.head: Dstillery and Canvas Worldwide Deploy DS-1 Agentic AI for Live In-Flight Campaign Optimization
stories.plain: Dstillery's DS-1 agent now makes real-time optimization decisions on active media buys — moving beyond deal and audience discovery into autonomous in-flight budget and targeting adjustments. This is a meaningful step: agentic AI is no longer just pre-campaign setup but actively managing media spend mid-flight, raising questions about human oversight and accountability in paid media.

stories.head: Stop Buying 'AI' — A Four-Part Framework for Evaluating Vendor Capabilities
stories.plain: The piece argues that the label 'AI-powered' is now meaningless in vendor pitches and offers a structured four-part framework to cut through it. For marketers evaluating martech purchases, asking what specific capability a product delivers — rather than what model it uses — leads to better decisions and smarter contract terms. Practically useful for anyone in procurement or stack evaluation.

stories.head: Shoppable's Universal Checkout Plugin Goes Live Inside ChatGPT for 900M Weekly Users
stories.plain: Shoppable is the first commerce infrastructure provider to put multi-retailer product discovery and cart-building directly inside ChatGPT via the Plugin Directory. With 900 million weekly ChatGPT users now able to buy without leaving the AI interface, this signals that the AI answer layer is becoming a direct commerce channel — shifting where brands need to be present and discoverable.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Agents Are Breaking Into Production — and Breaking Security
body: Agentic AI is no longer a demo: Dstillery is running autonomous optimization on live ad campaigns, DevRev agents share organizational memory across systems, and Monk agents are making real phone calls to collect debt. The shift from assistant to autonomous actor is happening faster than most teams anticipated.

That speed is opening dangerous gaps. Zenity Labs' AgentForger disclosure shows that a single click can hand an attacker full control of an AI agent operating inside your stack. The same autonomy that makes agents powerful — tool access, memory, the ability to act — makes them a novel insider-threat surface that traditional security models were not built to handle.

themes.head: AI Search Is Rewriting the Brand Visibility Game
body: ChatGPT and its rivals are becoming the first stop for product discovery, yet Martech.org's analysis finds no clear brand leader in most categories inside these interfaces. That is an enormous opportunity — and an enormous threat — because the ranking logic of AI search bears little resemblance to Google SEO.

Simultaneously, Shoppable's checkout plugin landing inside ChatGPT signals that the purchase funnel is collapsing into the AI interface itself. Brands that are not actively optimizing for AI-answer inclusion risk becoming, as one headline put it, invisible to AI search — losing ground to competitors that may not even be other brands.
ev: MarTech · website invisible to AI search

themes.head: Voice AI Gets Organizational Memory and Moves Upstream
body: Two launches in a single day — DevRev's shared-memory voice agents and Monk's AI phone collections — mark a qualitative leap: voice AI is no longer a single-session chatbot but a persistent, context-aware system that knows prior interactions across teams and touchpoints. RingCentral's deep partnership with OpenAI adds enterprise-grade infrastructure beneath this wave.

The business implication is that phone and voice channels, long treated as legacy cost centers, are being rebuilt as AI-native engagement layers. Decisions about telephony, CRM, and customer-service tooling are now inseparable from AI strategy — and the window to architect that integration deliberately is closing.

themes.head: Consumer Trust Deficit Is the Ceiling on AI ROI
body: Martech.org's research makes a counterintuitive argument: consumer distrust of AI is not primarily about the technology — it is about pre-existing distrust of the brands deploying it. Forrester's Gina Bhawalkar echoes this, warning that chasing AI speed at the expense of transparency is a compounding trust tax that quietly erodes long-term customer relationships.

This reframes the ROI calculation for AI martech. Capabilities that feel fast and efficient internally may register as intrusive or impersonal externally, especially when personalization crosses into surveillance-adjacent territory. Earning AI credibility requires the same brand fundamentals — consistency, honesty, relevance — that earned trust before AI existed.
ev: Agile Brand · Forrester on AI speed vs trust
MarTech · what CMOs should do about AI

themes.head: Buy Capabilities, Not Tools — the Procurement Frame Is Changing
body: A cluster of strategic pieces this month converges on one uncomfortable message: most martech buyers are still purchasing AI products when they should be purchasing outcomes and workflow capabilities. The distinction matters because point-solution AI purchases routinely fail to survive integration with legacy enterprise data and process silos — exactly what the Salesforce agentic stumble illustrates.

The corrective lens is 'unified workflows first.' Acceldata's European expansion around cross-lake data infrastructure, Paper's $34 million raise for agentic-era design, and Bluehost's small-business agentic platform all reflect vendors betting that horizontal capability layers — not vertical AI features — will win the next procurement cycle. Buyers who internalize this distinction will avoid shelfware; those who don't will repeat the last decade's martech regret.
ev: MarTech · unlocking AI through unified workflows

month.takeaway: Every major signal this month points toward the same inflection: the question is no longer whether to adopt AI but whether your trust, security, and data foundations can carry the weight of agents that now act, speak, and spend autonomously on your behalf.
